[[Kevin Henderson (rugby league)|Kevin Henderson]] (brother) }} }} '''Ian Henderson''' (born 23 April 1983) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[NRL]]. A [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international {{rlp|hk}}, his brothers, [[Andrew Henderson (rugby league)|Andrew Henderson]] and [[Kevin Henderson (rugby league)|Kevin]], are also international rugby league players., ==Early years== Henderson was born in [[Torquay]], [[Devon, England]] but his family later moved to the [[Central Coast, New South Wales|Central Coast]] of Australia and he attended [[Terrigal, New South Wales|Terrigal High School]]. ==Playing career== ===Sydney Roosters=== Henderson made his [[National Rugby League]] premiership début for the [[Sydney Roosters]] against the [[Parramatta Eels]] in round 1 of the [[2003 NRL season]]. After two years at the Sydney Roosters he moved to [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] however did not achieve more game time and left halfway through the [[2005 NRL season]]. He is sporadically called up to don the famous colours of 'Dinner Club', representing the local third division Oz Tag team and subsequently securing eligibility for their end of season trip. ===Bradford=== Henderson signed with the [[Bradford Bulls]] in 2005, where he made a massive impact in helping Bradford win the championship. He played for Bradford at hooker in their [[2005 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. As [[Super League]] champions, Bradford played [[National Rugby League]] premiers [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]]. Henderson started at hooker in the Bulls' 30–10 victory. With the arrival of [[Terry Newton]], Henderson found it hard to gain selection into the Bulls starting team.{{Citation needed|date=December 2007}} On 23 June 2007, the [[New Zealand Warriors]] announced they had signed Henderson for two years.{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/6233368.stm|title=Henderson set to leave Bradford|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-23|access-date=2007-06-25}}{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/06/23/RUGBYL_Bradford.html|title=Henderson to Quit Bulls|publisher=SportingLife|date=2007-06-23|access-date=2007-06-25|archive-date=30 September 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930055543/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/06/23/RUGBYL_Bradford.html|url-status=dead}} The Hendersons are a Scottish family and all three brothers have played for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] at international level.{{cite news|work=BBC Sport|title=Scotland call up Super League six|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/4282586.stm|access-date=2007-12-08|date=2005-09-26}}{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/05/29/RUGBYL_Scotland.html |title=Robertson ready for Scotland |publisher=Sportinglife |date=2008-05-30 }}{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23782306-23214,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121206035930/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23782306-23214,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=2012-12-06|title=Robertson set for kilt call-up|work=Fox Sports|date=2008-05-30|access-date=2008-05-30}} [[File:Ian Henderson.jpg|thumb|left|Henderson in 2011]] ===New Zealand Warriors=== Henderson made his début for the Warriors in round 1 2008, playing against the [[Melbourne Storm]]. While a Warriors player, Henderson said [[Castleford]], [[West Yorkshire]] was the worst place he had lived. He said that residents have rotten teeth, spend too much time drinking and need a bath.{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/uk_news/england/bradford/7688460.stm|title=Rugby star slams 'bad teeth' town|date=2008-10-24|access-date=2008-10-24|work=[[BBC News Online]]}} All three Henderson brothers featured in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Scotland|Scotland squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7587971.stm|title=Henderson trio in Scots cup squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-08-29|access-date=2008-10-03}} In May 2009 the Warriors announced they had re-signed him until the end of the 2011 season.[http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/54880/Henderson-re-signs-with-Warriors/Default.aspx Henderson re-signs with Warriors] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090517023302/http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/54880/Henderson-re-signs-with-Warriors/Default.aspx |date=17 May 2009 }} ''AAP'', 14 May 2009 [[File:Danny Brough and Ian Henderson 2013 RLWC.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Henderson (right) and [[Danny Brough]] playing for Scotland in [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] game against Italy.]] On 23 August, it was announced by the Warriors that they had released Henderson from the final year of his contract so he could sign a three-year deal with the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] starting in 2011.{{cite web|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/rugby-league-news/henderson-bids-adieu-warriors-3728432|title=Henderson bids adieu to Warriors|date=23 August 2010|work=[[Television New Zealand]]|access-date=4 October 2011}} ===Catalans Dragons=== Henderson joined the Catalans Dragons in 2011. Henderson made 129 appearances for Catalans between 2011 and 2015. Henderson was again part of Scotland's [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign. ===Return to the Sydney Roosters=== On 3 August 2015, Henderson announced his return to Australia to play for his former club, the [[Sydney Roosters]], in 2016. Henderson's move back to the NRL ended his five-year stay in France.{{cite news |title = Ian Henderson to leave Catalans join Sydney Roosters |publisher = Sky Sports |date = 2015-08-03 |url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/9933498/ian-henderson-to-leave-catalans-to-join-sydney-roosters |access-date = 2015-08-04}} On 17 March 2016, Henderson suffered a broken leg in the Roosters' 0–40 defeat by the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/news/roosters-lose-henderson-to-graphic-leg-injury-nrl/1t6rb6ehrvjxr165uk9ps79v6y|title=Graphic Content: Roosters lose Henderson to horrific leg injury}} This eventually lead him to announcing his retirement from rugby league later in the year. In 2018, Henderson returned to Rugby League on the Central Coast leading the Kincumber Colts. ==Commentary career== Henderson was a commentator at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] mainly featuring in Scotland's group stage matches as a co-commentator.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bbc-confirms-world-cup-punditry-team-including-james-graham-and-andrew-voss#:~:text=BBC's%20lead%20rugby%20league%20commentator,complete%20the%20commentary%20line%2Dup./|title=BBC confirms World Cup punditry team including James Graham and Andrew Voss|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20090203181437/http://warriors.co.nz/page/player_ian_henderson.html Ian Henderson Official Player Profile] *[http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/sportbullsheadlines/display.var.1436172.0.henderson_bent_on_revenge.php Henderson bent on revenge] ''The Telegraph and Argus'', 30 May 2007 {{Bradford Bulls - 2005 Super League Grand Final winners}} {{Bradford Bulls - 2006 World Club Challenge winners}} {{Scotland 2008 Rugby League World Cup squad}} {{Scotland 2013 Rugby League World Cup squad}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Henderson, Ian}} [[Category:1983 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Auckland rugby league team players]] [[Category:Bradford Bulls players]] [[Category:Catalans Dragons players]] [[Category:English people of Scottish descent]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:Henderson family (rugby league)|Ian]] [[Category:New Zealand Warriors players]] [[Category:Parramatta Eels players]] [[Category:Rugby league hookers]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Torquay]] [[Category:Scotland national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Sydney Roosters players]]
